title: TC-FALCON-CEQ-001: Custom EQ Save Button Commit — Falcon Device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the Falcon device name to enter Device Screen.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom' tab to enter Custom EQ screen.
Verify the Save button is disabled/grayed out (sliders at default positions).
Move any EQ slider (e.g., 100Hz band) from 0 to +6 dB.
Verify the Save button becomes enabled (changes color, becomes tappable).
Tap the Save button.
Observe any visual confirmation (toast, button state change, or screen flash).
Tap the X close button (top-left) to return to Device Settings.
Navigate back: Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom tab.
Observe the 100Hz slider value.
expected: The 100Hz slider should still show +6 dB. The Save button should be disabled (since no changes were made since last save). The custom EQ values are confirmed to have been committed to persistent storage on the Falcon device after pressing Save.

title: TC-FALCON-CEQ-002: Save Button State Machine — Default → Modified → Saved → Return Verification
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Falcon device.
Navigate: Device Screen → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Record all default slider positions (all should be at 0 dB).
Verify Save button is disabled.
Move the 250Hz slider to +4 dB and the 1kHz slider to -3 dB.
Verify Save button is now enabled.
Tap Save button.
Verify Save button returns to disabled state (no pending changes).
Tap the X close button to exit Equalizer screen.
Re-enter: Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Check the 250Hz slider shows +4 dB and the 1kHz slider shows -3 dB.
Check Save button is disabled (values match last saved state).
expected: The Save button state machine should transition correctly: disabled at default → enabled after slider move → disabled after Save press. The saved values should persist after exiting and re-entering the screen on Falcon device.

title: TC-FALCON-CEQ-003: Save Button vs. X Close Button Behavior Asymmetry — Falcon Device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Falcon device.
Navigate: Device Screen → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Move the 8kHz slider to +5 dB.
Do NOT press Save.
Tap the X close button to exit Equalizer screen.
Re-enter: Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Observe the 8kHz slider value.
Now move the 8kHz slider to -2 dB.
Tap the Save button.
Tap the X close button to exit.
Re-enter: Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Observe the 8kHz slider value.
expected: After step 6 (X close without Save), the slider may or may not persist depending on whether X close auto-saves. After step 12 (Save then X close), the slider MUST show -2 dB. This test reveals the asymmetry: if X close auto-saves but Save button does not, the bug is exposed because step 6 will show persistence but step 12 will show the value reverted to +5 dB (or default).

title: TC-FALCON-CEQ-004: Custom EQ Save with Deep Lifecycle — App Kill and Bluetooth Reconnect on Falcon
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Falcon device.
Navigate: Device Screen → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Move the 500Hz slider to +3 dB and the 4kHz slider to +2 dB.
Tap the Save button.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recent apps).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for automatic reconnection to Falcon device (or manually reconnect).
Navigate: Device Screen → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Observe the 500Hz and 4kHz slider values.
Move the 500Hz slider to +6 dB.
Tap Save.
Turn off Falcon device Bluetooth (or power off device).
Turn on Falcon device and wait for reconnection.
Navigate: Device Screen → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Observe the 500Hz and 4kHz slider values.
expected: After app kill and relaunch (step 9), the sliders should show +3 dB and +2 dB — the values that were saved. After Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle (step 15), the sliders should show +6 dB and +2 dB — confirming the second Save also persisted through device power cycle on Falcon.

title: TC-FALCON-CEQ-005: Custom EQ Save — Multiple Slider Movements Before Single Save
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Falcon device.
Navigate: Device Screen → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Move 100Hz slider to +2 dB.
Move 250Hz slider to +4 dB.
Move 500Hz slider to -1 dB.
Move 1kHz slider to +3 dB.
Move 2kHz slider to +5 dB.
Move 4kHz slider to -2 dB.
Move 8kHz slider to +1 dB.
Verify Save button is enabled.
Tap Save button.
Navigate away: tap X close button.
Re-enter: Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Verify ALL seven slider positions match the values set in steps 3-9.
expected: All seven EQ band slider values should persist correctly after pressing Save once. No slider should revert to default. The Save button should commit the entire multi-band configuration atomically on Falcon device.

title: TC-FALCON-CEQ-006: Custom EQ Save — Rapid Successive Saves
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Falcon device.
Navigate: Device Screen → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Move 100Hz slider to +3 dB.
Tap Save.
Immediately move 100Hz slider to +6 dB.
Tap Save.
Immediately move 100Hz slider to 0 dB.
Tap Save.
Navigate away: tap X close button.
Re-enter: Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Observe the 100Hz slider value.
Move 100Hz slider to -3 dB.
Tap Save.
Force-kill the app and relaunch.
Reconnect to Falcon and navigate to Custom EQ.
Observe the 100Hz slider value.
expected: After step 10, the 100Hz slider should show 0 dB (the last saved value). After step 15, the 100Hz slider should show -3 dB. Rapid successive saves should each independently commit to persistent storage without race conditions on Falcon device.

title: TC-FALCON-CEQ-007: Custom EQ Save — Preset Switch After Save Clears Custom Values Correctly
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Falcon device.
Navigate: Device Screen → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Move 250Hz slider to +5 dB and 2kHz slider to +4 dB.
Tap Save.
Switch to 'Bass Boost' preset.
Verify sliders change to Bass Boost curve.
Switch back to 'Custom' tab.
Verify 250Hz shows +5 dB and 2kHz shows +4 dB.
Now switch to 'Voice Clarity' preset.
Switch back to 'Custom' tab.
Verify saved custom values are still intact.
expected: Switching away from Custom to a preset and back should preserve the last saved custom EQ values. The Save button commit should survive preset switching on Falcon device.

title: TC-FALCON-CEQ-008: Custom EQ Save — Device Firmware Readback Verification (Advanced)
steps: Connect Falcon device to a test setup capable of reading device EQ parameters via AT commands or firmware API.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Falcon device.
Navigate: Device Screen → Audio Settings → Equalizer → Custom.
Read and record current device EQ parameters via firmware API (baseline).
Move 100Hz slider to +4 dB and 8kHz slider to -3 dB.
Tap Save button.
Immediately read device EQ parameters via firmware API.
Compare API-read values with UI slider positions.
Navigate away and back to Custom EQ screen.
Read device EQ parameters via firmware API again.
Compare all three data points: UI after save, API after save, API after re-entry.
expected: The firmware API should return EQ values matching the UI slider positions (+4 dB at 100Hz, -3 dB at 8kHz) immediately after Save and after re-entry. This confirms the Save button actually writes to the Falcon device firmware, not just the app UI layer.
